data.title: IVD Medical Plans Web3 Drug-Asset Exchange to Launch Stablecoin
data.description:
IVD Medical said it was pursuing a web3 exchange focused on tokenizing its intellectual property and planned to issue its own cryptocurrency, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the medical equipment seller jumped over 7% in morning trade Friday.

The company said it planned to build a so-called real-world asset tokenization trading platform based on its drug pipeline, and that the IVDD stablecoin will serve as the transaction medium for trading the tokenized drug assets.

The company is preparing to seek regulatory approval in Hong Kong for a stablecoin license as well as in the U.S. where it also plans to seek a share listing.

data.title: HK-listed Luk Fook climbs on quarterly sales growth
data.description:
** Shares of jewellery chain operator Luk Fook Holdings (International) climb 4.4% to HK$22.3, their highest since June 16

** Hong Kong-based jeweller said its overall same store sales increased 5% y/y for three-month quarter ended in June 2025, of which China up 19% while Hong Kong and Macau up 3%

** Although the spike in gold prices may affect sales performance, an increase in profit margin will help mitigate the impact of the decline in sales - Co

** Recent business improvements indicate that consumers are gradually adapting to high gold prices, suggesting a potential normalisation of gold sales - Co

** Hang Seng Consumption Index (.HSCGSI) gains 0.8%, Hang Seng Index adds 0.9%

** YTD, stock up 53.1%

data.title: Jinduicheng Molybdenum's H1 Profit Falls 9%, Revenue Climbs 6%
data.description:
Jinduicheng Molybdenum's net profit attributable to shareholders in the first half fell 8.5% to 13.8 billion yuan from 15.1 billion yuan a year earlier, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

Earnings per share slipped 8.4% year on year to 0.428 yuan from 0.467 yuan.

The Chinese molybdenum producer's total operating revenue climbed 5.6% to 69.6 billion yuan from 65.9 billion yuan in the preceding year, the filing said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Saudi Aramco Said to Near $10 Billion Investment from BlackRock-led Group
data.description:
Saudi Arabian Oil Co. (SASE:2222), d/b/a Saudi Aramco, is reportedly close to securing a $10 billion investment for its Jafurah gas project from a BlackRock-led group.

The investment would likely focus on the state-owned petroleum and natural gas company's project infrastructure, including pipelines and a gas processing plant, Reuters reported on Thursday, citing two sources familiar with the matter.

The transaction's structure is expected to be similar to two infrastructure deals agreed upon by the companies in 2021, where investors earned revenue from tariffs from Aramco, while the latter retained control of the infrastructure.

BlackRock declined to comment, while Saudi Aramco did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: RAK Insurance Logs Higher H1 Profit, Insurance Revenue
data.description:
Ras Al Khaimah National Insurance (ADX:RAKNIC), d/b/a RAK Insurance, said Thursday its profit and insurance revenue in the first half increased year over year.

Profit after tax for the six months ended June 30 was 24.1 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 1.74 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.20 dirham from 0.01 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurance company's insurance revenue was 255.4 million dirhams, compared with 243.6 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: RAS Technology Holdings Chairman to Retire
data.description:
RAS Technology Holdings said that Non-Executive Chair Kate Carnell will retire, effective July 18, as the company shifts focus toward accelerating the global growth of its wagering technology, according to a same-day filing with the Australian bourse.

The board has appointed Non-Executive Director Greg Nichols as interim chair while the search for a permanent chair continues, the filing said.

data.title: TSMC Shares on Track for Record Close After Earnings Beat
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s shares are on track to close at a record high after it delivered an earnings beat and raised revenue guidance for 2025 despite multiple headwinds.

Shares of the world's largest contract chip maker in Taipei were up 2.2% Friday morning at 1,155 New Taiwan Dollars, equivalent to US$39.25. TSMC's shares have climbed steadily over the past three months and are set to surpass the previous high reached in January. Shares had taken a hit earlier this year amid concerns about the sustainability of AI spending and U.S. President Trump's tariff policy.

The company on Thursday reported a 61% jump in second-quarter net profit from a year earlier to 398.27 billion New Taiwan dollars, equivalent to US$13.57 billion, topping market expectations. Its revenue rose 39% to NT$933.79 billion.

The chip maker raised its guidance for 2025 revenue growth to around 30% from a mid-20% rise projected earlier despite headwinds from U.S. tariffs and unfavorable foreign exchange rates.

According to analysts, TSMC's commanding lead in chip making and advanced packaging technology should help sustain its industry leadership and long-term growth.

Management highlighted in a post-earnings call on Thursday that the capacity for both advanced chips and advanced packaging remains tight versus demand and it is trying to narrow the gap.

Its next-generation 2-nanometer chips, scheduled for mass production later this year, would require substantial manufacturing complexity and highly refined 3D process control, Citi analysts said in a research note.

TSMC's long-standing expertise in process control, which is one of its core competitive advantages, should support selling its value, the analysts added.

Meanwhile, DBS analyst Jim Au said the market hasn't fully appreciated TSMC's advanced packaging technology, which could become a major profit driver in the coming years. As chips grow smaller, finding smarter ways to integrate different types of chips has become a more important way to improve performance.

While U.S. tariff policy uncertainties have clouded the company's outlook, TSMC chief executive C.C. Wei has downplayed the tariff impact on its business. On Thursday, he reiterated that the company hadn't seen any changes in customers' behavior.

"TSMC is undervalued as the market is overestimating tariff effects and underestimating the longevity of AI investments," Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee said, adding that Taiwan could reach a trade deal with the U.S. before the August 1 deadline.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Vinci Energies to Buy German Industrial Group R+S
data.description:
French concessions and construction company Vinci said Thursday its Vinci Energies unit agreed to buy R+S Group, which provides electrical installation, automation, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning services in Germany.

Completion of the acquisition is subject to the approval of German competition authorities. If the transaction is approved, R+S will be integrated into the Building Solutions division of Vinci Energies.

data.title: Genesis Minerals Price Target Cut 3.3% to A$4.35/Share by Ord Minnett
New Zealand, Canada to Resolve Dairy Dispute under CPTPP
data.description:
New Zealand and Canada agreed to resolve a dairy dispute under the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP), Trade and Investment Minister Todd McClay said in a media release Friday.

Canada has pledged to alter its dairy quota administration under the CPTPP, including reallocating underused quotas and imposing penalties for importers who misuse them, the release said.

New Zealand initiated dispute settlement proceedings against Canada over restrictive dairy export access under the CPTPP in 2022, according to the release.

data.title: Gold Road Resources Price Target Cut 2.7% to A$3.30/Share by Euroz Hartleys
US FDA Committee Advises Against Blenrep Combinations for Myeloma
data.description:
The US Food and Drug Administration's Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee voted against GSK's Blenrep combinations, citing concerns over the overall benefit/risk profile at the proposed dosage.

The pharmaceutical company said Thursday that it will continue working with the FDA as the US regulator concludes its Blenrep review, taking the committee's recommendation into account, before the July 23 Prescription Drug User Fee Act date.

The combinations of Blenrep, generically named belantamab mafodotin, were tested in adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who had received at least one prior line of therapy. Blenrep combinations are approved for the indication in the UK, Japan, Switzerland and other markets, with applications under review in the European Union and China.

data.title: Energy Transition Minerals Unit Secures Preferred Bidder Status for Spain Mine
data.description:
Energy Transition Minerals said its Spanish unit was recommended as the preferred bidder by a court-appointed administrator for the Penouta Mine in Spain, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

A final auction is set for July 29, with a court decision

expected by October, the filing said.

ETM's offer totals up to AU$3.6 million along with an AU$900,000 deposit.

The company may also cover up to AU$2.2 million in care and maintenance costs before closing, targeted by the end of the year.

data.title: Japan Inflation Eases, But BOJ Still Faces Policy Challenges — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--A slight easing in Japan's consumer inflation is welcome news for the central bank, but stubbornly high food prices will be of concern for policymakers whose hands remain tied by tariffs.

Core consumer inflation, which excludes volatile fresh food prices, rose 3.3% from a year earlier in June, government data showed Friday. That compared with May's 3.7% rise, and matched the forecast in a poll of economists by data provider Quick.

The slowdown was due largely to cooler energy prices, which climbed 2.9%--much slower than the 8.1% increase seen in May as government gasoline subsidies kept price growth in check.

"Price pressures are cooling in Japan, which is very welcome," said Krishna Bhimavarapu, an economist at State Street Investment Management. "Even though we expect another hike from the Bank of Japan this year, our conviction is weakened."

While persistent inflation has been hurting consumer appetite, it may not immediately trigger an interest-rate increase by the central bank because of concerns about the impact of higher U.S. tariffs on the economy.

President Trump has threatened to impose a "reciprocal" tariff of 25% on Japanese exports starting on Aug. 1. Duties on autos--a core industry for Japan--are already in effect. Tokyo has been trying to negotiate a trade deal with Washington, but progress has been difficult.

The lack of clarity on tariffs complicates matters for the BOJ, which is also watching sticky price pressures, particularly in food.

Friday's data showed no sign of easing in food inflation in June, with rice prices still up around 100% on the year despite government efforts to bring down the cost of the staple by releasing reserves. Excluding fresh items, food prices increased 8.2%, compared with May's 7.7% rise.

That's unlikely to be of much comfort to the ruling coalition, which is facing a key electoral test this weekend.

Rising living costs are a central issue in the Upper House election scheduled for Sunday. Under pressure to maintain its majority, the ruling Liberal Democratic Party has promised a cash handout of 20,000 yen per person, or about $134. Opposition parties are calling for cuts to the consumption tax.

NLI Research Institute's Taro Saito expects core consumer inflation to fall below 3% in August due primarily to the slowdown in energy price growth and remain around the central bank's 2% target for the rest of the year.

"Businesses may be growing less resistant to price increases, after experiencing persistent inflation and the passing on of labor and logistics costs," Saito said, adding that food inflation in particular is expected to remain high for some time.

The BOJ's policy board is scheduled to release fresh price projections at its next meeting on July 30-31. Many economists say the central bank will likely keep policy settings steady again this month, even though it's not done tightening yet.

Judging solely by consumer price trends, the BOJ will likely be in a position to consider additional rate hikes at least through 2025, said Mizuho Securities economist Ryosuke Katagi.

As reaffirmed by the latest CPI data, domestic prices--especially for goods--remain strong, but with the conclusion of U.S.-Japan trade negotiations still uncertain, the BOJ will likely continue to cautiously assess the timing of interest-rate hikes, Katagi said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Philippines posts $226 mln balance of payment surplus in June
data.description:
The Philippines' overall balance of payments (BOP) position was a surplus of $226 million in June after a deficit of $298 million in May, the central bank said on Friday.

The cumulative BOP level for the first six months was a deficit of $5.6 billion. The central bank has forecast a $6.3 billion deficit for 2025.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Materials Sector Climbs, Leads All-Sector Rally
data.description:
The materials sector jumped almost 2% at midday Friday, leading a broad-based rally, as all sectors on the Australian Securities Exchange were trading in the green.

On Wall Street, both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite indices closed at record highs on Thursday, up 0.5% and 0.7%, respectively.

The S&P/ASX 200 index was up 1.1%.

BHP Group , which was up almost 3%, reported copper production of 516,200 tonnes in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, up 2% year-over-year and 1% from the prior quarter. Iron ore output was 70.3 million tonnes, up 2% from a year earlier and 14% from the fiscal third quarter.

In fiscal 2025, the company produced 2 million tonnes of copper and 263 million tonnes of iron ore, up 8% and 1%, respectively, from a year earlier.

data.title: BHP Group Logs Higher FY25 Production of Copper, Iron Ore; Steelmaking, Energy Coal Output Drops
data.description:
BHP Group (, BHG.JO) said Friday that production of copper and iron ore increased in fiscal 2025, while steelmaking coal, energy coal, and nickel output declined year over year.

For the 12 months ended June 30, copper production grew to 2,016,700 tonnes from 1,865,000 tonnes in the prior year, and iron ore output moved to 263 million tonnes from 260 million tonnes.

On the other hand, steelmaking coal production fell to 18 million tonnes from 22.3 million tonnes in the previous year, while energy coal output declined to 15 million tonnes from 15.4 million tonnes. Nickel production in Western Australia plunged to 30,200 tonnes from 81,600 tonnes a year ago.

data.title: Market Chatter: EQT, Omers, Phoenix Tower Mull Offers for Cellnex Telecom's Swiss Arm
data.description:
EQT , Omers Administration and Phoenix Tower are exploring final proposals for the acquisition of a majority stake in Cellnex Telecom's Swiss business, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Binding offers are expected to be submitted by the end of the week, the people said, noting that the business could be valued at up to 2 billion euros. The sources said deliberations between the parties continue, but the suitors may still decide against lodging a proposal.

Cellnex currently owns 72% of the business, with Swiss Life's Swiss Life Asset Managers holding the remaining interest.

Cellnex, EQT, Omers, and Phoenix did not immediately respond to requests for comments from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

10:00 - German finance minister and bundesbank president speak on the sidelines of G20 meeting - German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil and Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel speak on the sidelines of the G20 meeting in Durban.

10:30 - German Chancellor Merz holds news conference in Berlin - German Chancellor Friedrich Merz holds a news conference on domestic and foreign policy issues in Berlin. He expected to cover a wide range of topics before the summer break, including the economy, tariffs, migration and Russia’s war in Ukraine.

11:15 - European interior ministers meet on Germany’s highest peak to discuss migration policies - Top European migration officials gather on Germany’s highest mountain, the Zugspitze, to discuss stricter migration measures and a speedier integration of migrants into societies. The event is organized by German Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt, who invited his counterparts from France, Poland, Austria, Denmark and the Czech Republic.

data.title: Lynas Rare Earths Price Target Cut 10% to A$7.80/Share by Ord Minnett
Lynas Rare Earths Downgraded to Sell from Hold by Ord Minnett
Sing Holdings, Sunway Developments Win Singapore Site in URA Tender; Shares Up Nearly 4%
data.description:
Sing Holdings , through its subsidiary, Sing Holdings Residential, and Sunway Developments, secured a land parcel on Chuan Grove in Singapore, awarded by the city-state's Urban Redevelopment Authority, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the company were up nearly 4% in recent trading.

The partners will now form a joint venture company to complete the acquisition and will undertake a residential development on the site.

data.title: Burgundy Diamond Mines Downgraded to Hold from Speculative Buy by Euroz Hartleys
Xiamen Tungsten's H1 Profit Slips 4%, Revenue Climbs 12%
data.description:
Xiamen Tungsten's net profit attributable to shareholders in the first half slipped 4.4% to 971.8 million yuan from 1.02 billion yuan a year earlier, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

Earnings per share dropped 15% year on year to 0.61 yuan from 0.72 yuan.

The Chinese tungsten carbide manufacturer's total operating revenue climbed 12% to 19.2 billion yuan from 17.2 billion yuan in the previous year, the disclosure said.

The company's shares rose less than 2% during morning trade.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei edges down, off near 2-week high amid election outcome worries
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average slipped from a more than two-week high to trade lower on Friday as investors weighed the outcome of the nation's upper house election due on the weekend.

The Nikkei slipped 0.31% to 39,778.85 by the midday break. Earlier, it had risen to as much as 40,087.59, its highest level since July 1, underpinned by the strong performance of Wall Street, but fell soon as investors started selling to book profits.

The S&P 500 stock index and the Nasdaq Composite both finished at record highs on Thursday, as investors embraced strong economic data and earnings reports that showed American consumers remained willing to spend.

For the week, the Nikkei is set to rise 0.5% and snap a two straight weeks of losses.

The broader Topix was down 0.13% to 2,836.1.

"Investors did not want to take a risk in buying stocks ahead of the national election on the weekend," said Yugo Tsuboi, chief strategist at Daiwa Securities.

"But the momentum is not bad as about half the stocks rose."

Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba's Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) and its partner Komeito are expected to lose their majority in the upper house on Sunday.

Strategists now focus on whether Ishiba will remain in his position or step down after the election, as a gauge of the nation's potential policy shift, which could lead to a cut in the national consumption tax.

Chip-related heavyweight fell, with Advantest and Tokyo Electron losing 4.27% and 0.7%, respectively.

Disco tanked 10.26% to become the biggest percentage loser on the Nikkei, as the chipmaking device supplier's quarterly operating profit forecast missed market expectations.

Uniqlo-brand owner Fast Retailing rose 0.79% to provide the biggest support to the Nikkei. Technology investor SoftBank Group rose 1.5%.

Of more than 1,600 stocks on the Tokyo Stock Exchange's prime section, 42% rose and 53% fell, and 4% traded flat.

data.title: Merrill Lynch (Singapore) Purchases 4.5 Million Shares in NTT DC REIT; Shares Down Nearly 1%
data.description:
Merrill Lynch (Singapore) purchased 4,451,600 shares in NTT DC REIT at a price range of 0.970 to 0.985 apiece, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the REIT were down nearly 1% in recent trading.

The purchase was made on July 17 as principal or through dealers on Merrill Lynch's behalf, the filing added.

data.title: Tiantan Biological Products Starts Phase Three Trial for IV Immunoglobulin
data.description:
Beijing Tiantan Biological Products subsidiary Chengdu Rongsheng Pharma has initiated a phase three trial for its intravenous immunoglobulin to treat chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy.

The trial follows ethics approval and pre-enrollment preparations, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Burgundy Diamond Mines Price Target Cut 52% to A$0.12/Share by Euroz Hartleys
